## 2026.7.0-alpha.0 (July 2026)

### Minor Changes

- #2032 `25272e5` Thanks @vcua_sfemu! - Add a single `app.cookies.domain` knob for the storefront cookie domain

    A new global config field, `app.cookies.domain`, sets the default `Domain` on **every** cookie the storefront writes — the auth/session cookies and the site-context cookies (`site_id`, locale, currency). Set it once via `PUBLIC__app__cookies__domain=.example.com` instead of redeclaring the entire `commerce.sites` array just to share cookies across subdomains.

    Precedence (applies to all storefront cookies): per-site `commerce.sites[].cookies.domain` → global `app.cookies.domain` → host-only scoping (the default; setting a domain is opt-in). The SDK site-context middleware now applies the resolved per-site domain when serializing the site-context cookies, so the per-site override reaches them too.

    Also fixes the stale `PUBLIC__app__site__cookies__domain` references in the docs (the path never existed).

- #1932 `35d961b` Thanks @mjuraschik_sfemu! - w:

    Embedded Content Blocks (ECB)

    Adds support for fetching and rendering Page Designer components outside
    of a page context. The header is wired up as the first embedded component,
    hosting an `announcement` region for the new announcement banner. Data
    collection for embedded components reuses the same `componentData` promise
    map that pages use, so streaming and per-component Suspense boundaries
    work the same way for both.

- #2051 `606762a` Thanks @seckardt_sfemu! - Add context-aware revalidation for localized SCAPI resource-route fetchers.

    React Router does not surface the evaluated fetcher's identity to a generic resource route's `shouldRevalidate`, so it cannot scope revalidation per-fetcher. `useScapiFetcher` now registers each mounted fetcher's `load` under its encoded `[client, method, options]` key via `createFetcherRegistration(key)` — whose handle exposes `register(…)` and `unregister()` — and the route's `shouldRevalidate` drives reloads from there: on the first call of a mutation-triggered pass it walks the registry, reloads exactly the fetchers a declarative policy (`src/lib/routes/revalidation/api-client.ts`) says the mutation invalidates, and returns `false` to opt out of React Router's blanket auto-revalidation.

    Only `load` is tracked: the registry reloads fetchers, and replaying a `submit` would re-issue a write, so a fetcher's `submit` is never recorded. The shipped policy reloads every fetcher after a global mutation (`setSiteContext`, `updateShopperContext`), and — with the BOPIS extension — reloads store-scoped product fetchers (those reading `query.inventoryIds`) after a store-selection mutation (`setSelectedStore`, `cartPickupStoreUpdate`), since switching the store changes which inventory record a product call reads. Cart mutations do not reload product fetchers: SCAPI reserves inventory at order creation, not on basket changes, so adding to cart never moves a product's availability. Anything else does not reload; a new mutation→fetcher relationship is a single policy entry.

- #2042 `c0b87fb` Thanks @j-sheth_sfemu! - Shopper agent (Embedded Messaging) now contributes its exact configured origins to the CSP instead of relying on broad wildcards. The security middleware accepts boot-static CSP contributors; the template ships a shopper-agent contributor that derives script/frame/connect origins from `commerceAgent` config. The SDK now exports `normalizeCspOrigin` / `inspectCspOrigin` — the single source of truth for CSP-origin safety rules, shared by the boot-time contributor validator and the template normalizer so they cannot drift. Adds the `add-csp-contributor` skill for authoring new contributors. No change to shipped headers when shopper agent is disabled.

- #1937 `6c57057` Thanks @joel-uong_sfemu! - Auto-discover extension config files. An extension can now ship a `src/extensions/<name>/config.ts` that default-exports its client-side config defaults, and the framework discovers it at build time, merges it into `config.app.extension.<camelCaseFolder>`, and derives the TypeScript type automatically — no edits to `config.server.ts` or `src/types/config.ts`. Merchants override per environment with `PUBLIC__app__extension__<key>__<setting>`. Adds the `sfnext config aggregate-extensions` CLI prestep (run by `pnpm dev`, `pnpm build`, and `pnpm typecheck`), mirroring the existing extension locale aggregation.

- #2033 `2d8baef` Thanks @daniel-diaz_sfemu! - Neutralize shadcn shape tokens via --ui-radius, --ui-shadow, --ui-border-width overrides, enabling per-vertical theming of Card corners, elevation, and border widths without editing component source

- #1900 `39b9c15` Thanks @j-sheth_sfemu! - Wire SDK security-headers middleware into the React Router middleware chain. Apply per-request CSP nonce to the inline `__APP_CONFIG__` script. New customer-facing doc: `docs/README-SECURITY-HEADERS.md`.

- #1964 `4fe15ff` Thanks @joel-uong_sfemu! - Auto-discover server-only extension config. Parallel to the existing `config.ts` aggregator, an extension can now ship a `src/extensions/<name>/server-config.ts` that default-exports its server-only defaults — vendor SCAPI overrides, retry budgets, internal endpoints — and the framework merges them into `config.app.serverExtension.<camelCaseFolder>`, reachable via `getConfig(context).serverExtension.<key>` from server loaders, actions, and middleware. Values never reach the browser: the root loader strips `app.serverExtension` before React Router serializes the SSR hydration payload, and a Vite plugin fails the build if any client chunk imports the generated barrel. The existing `sfnext config aggregate-extensions` CLI now writes both barrels (`index.ts` and `server.ts`) in one run via `Promise.all` — no second command, no second prestep. There is intentionally no `PUBLIC__` override path — the same AST validator forbids `process.env`, so true secrets must continue to be read from `process.env` in a server route.

- #1866 `4238f07` Thanks @kumaravinash_sfemu! - Add `action.place-order-prepare` and `action.place-order-finalize` so payment extensions plug in via the `onPlaceOrder` ref without owning non-payment checkout work. The storefront's Place Order click handler now POSTs to `prepare` (basket validation, multiship resolution, totals refresh), awaits the extension's callback for createOrder + PSP confirmation, then POSTs to `finalize` (shopper-scope check, profile saves, basket teardown, navigation).

- #2033 `2d8baef` Thanks @daniel-diaz_sfemu! - Sync `main` into the multi-vertical template (`packages/template/`) to bring it current with the June 2026 main release. The catch-up was done in two steps: a conflict-free `git merge origin/main` (which carries main's SDK, the `template-retail-rsc-app` baseline, CI, and root scripts), followed by `sync-from-template-retail` to propagate the refreshed upstream into the `packages/template/` overlay.

    Resolved 26 overlay conflicts preserving the multi-vertical invariants: shadcn shape tokens kept authoritative (upstream `rounded-none`/`shadow-none` literals stripped from primitives so per-vertical shape resolves through `--ui-*`), `deepMerge` locale-override wrappers retained, locale `product.json` fold absorbed (mirror `renderLocaleIndex` updated to match), `config:aggregate-extensions` build prestep adopted now that the merged SDK provides it, and the cosmetic header (`announcementSlot` prop) + PDP override (`primaryCategoryId` page-fetch, relocated `einstein-recommenders` import) updated to absorb the canonical contract changes.

- #1978 `d4387fe` Thanks @d-phan_sfemu! - Add Turnstile `log-only` verification mode for safe production rollout (#1978)

    `security.turnstile.verification.mode` now accepts `'enforce'` (default), `'log-only'`, and `'disabled'`. In `log-only` mode the full siteverify pipeline runs but the function always returns `true` — no shopper is blocked. The would-be decision is logged at `info` level with `would_block: true|false` for aggregation in CloudWatch / Splunk.

    Set via env var: `PUBLIC__app__security__turnstile__verification__mode=log-only`.

    The existing `TURNSTILE_VERIFICATION_ENABLED` boolean is deprecated in favour of `mode` but continues to work unchanged.

- #1977 `ea714d4` Thanks @joel-uong_sfemu! - Type-narrow `useConfig()` so server-only namespaces are unreachable from client modules. The SDK now exposes a second augmentation slot, `ClientFacingAppConfigShape`, parallel to `AppConfigShape`; templates fill it with the shape they want `useConfig()` to return (typically `Omit<AppConfig, 'serverExtension'>`). The retail template wires it from its existing `ClientAppConfig` (= `Omit<AppConfig, ServerOnlyNamespace>`), so the runtime extractor (`extractClientConfig`) and the new type narrow read from the same source — adding a server-only namespace to `SERVER_ONLY_NAMESPACES` updates both. `useConfig().serverExtension` is now a TypeScript error in addition to the existing runtime stripping and Vite-build guards. `getConfig()` is unchanged: server callers correctly read `.serverExtension`. Customers who don't fill `ClientFacingAppConfigShape` see no behavior change on upgrade — `useConfig()` falls back to the full `AppConfigShape`.

- #1913 `8c9ff2f` Thanks @jcesarpierre_sfemu! - Update `.storybook/README-STORYBOOK.md` to match the actual repo structure: rewrite Project Structure section with three real folder shapes (Single component, Multi-component, flat shadcn primitives), align tree diagram with the real `src/components/` and `.storybook/` layout, and simplify the Basic Story Structure example.

- #1928 `93d70af` Thanks @j-sheth_sfemu! - Align README-AUTH.md with the server-only auth implementation and fix stale `auth.server.ts` comments

- #1976 `315eb50` Thanks @alafemina_sfemu! - Add `sfcc.orderConfirmation.shipping.details` UITarget wrapping the shipping details section on the order confirmation page, enabling extensions to replace or augment the entire shipping display

- #2018 `abca118` Thanks @alafemina_sfemu! - Update express checkout placeholder to match UX spec: correct GPay/PayPal/Venmo logos, Venmo brand color (#008CFF), and "or continue below" divider text

- #2067 `468a61e` Thanks @kumaravinash_sfemu! - Fix shipping promotion strikethrough showing identical prices before a method is submitted. The strikethrough now only renders when the basket's `priceAdjustments` confirm an actual discount is applied.

- #1867 `e751c22` Thanks @seckardt_sfemu! - **Prevent CloudFront from caching server errors on data requests.** A new middleware sets a caching header on server-error responses (status 500–504) so CloudFront doesn't keep serving a stale error after the underlying problem is fixed. Most of these errors are marked "do not store" — except 503, which is allowed to cache for just two seconds. A 503 signals that the backend is temporarily overloaded (SCAPI load shedding), so a very short shared cache acts as a system-wide cool-down that shields the origin from a retry stampede while still clearing within seconds. The behavior is scoped to React Router single-fetch data interactions (the client-side navigations and fetcher calls that hit `.data` URLs and return a turbo-stream payload); full-page document responses are left untouched, so CloudFront's default error caching still acts as a backoff that shields the origin during traffic spikes. A data interaction is detected from either the `.data` request URL suffix or React Router's single-fetch response marker, so detection keeps working both today and after a future React Router upgrade preserves the suffix on the request. Note: this also requires a configuration change on the CDN side (setting the error caching TTL to zero for those status codes), because the CDN won't fully respect the origin's caching headers on its own.

- #2028 `ec0a077` Thanks @ssojka_sfemu! - Improve the Page Designer page-resolution debug log. The `resolvedContext` entry now preserves each resolved data binding's `type → id → field → value` structure instead of collapsing bindings to a list of field names, truncating long string values to a reasonable length so the log conveys the content without emitting large payloads. Tracked under.

- #1886 `953164d` Thanks @seckardt_sfemu! - Add observability log lines for two otherwise-hard-to-track SCAPI failure categories. This is detection and logging only — no automatic retries.
    - **SCAPI under heavy load (load shedding).** A new wrapper inspects a special header that SCAPI includes in its responses to indicate how busy it is. When the server advertises pressure on a request it still serves, a warning is logged; when the server actually rejects the request with a 503, an error is logged. Both include the request URL (with sensitive parts masked) and the load percentage when available.
    - **Rate limiting (HTTP 429).** When SCAPI responds with a 429 ("too many requests"), an error is logged including the request URL and the raw `Retry-After` value the server sent (if any).

    Both share a common `SCAPI health` marker in the log message so they can be pulled from logs with a single search. The logs only fire for actual network responses — not for results served from a local cache.

- #2011 `95bb39d` Thanks @jbisa_sfemu! - Fix the contact-info Continue button staying disabled in two cases that blocked checkout E2E scenarios on the MRT pool target. (1) The button no longer gates on `isSendingOtp`, so the email-blur authorize fetcher being in flight at click-time on slow networks no longer locks the button — `otpFlowActiveRef` already prevents step advancement until the fetcher settles, so the OTP/login modal flows are unaffected. (2) The button no longer gates on `!form.formState.isValid` either; React Hook Form's `handleSubmit` already short-circuits on invalid forms and the inline field errors give the shopper feedback. Removing this gate also unblocks validation E2E scenarios that intentionally submit invalid input to assert error rendering. Re-enables 34 E2E checkout scenarios across 10 spec files that were skipped under.

- #1896 `bd94971` Thanks @seckardt_sfemu! - refactor(cart): extract bonus modal slot computation to a tested helper

    Moves the inline IIFE that computes `bonusDiscountSlots` for `BonusProductModal` out of `CartContent`'s JSX and into `getBonusDiscountSlotsForPromotion(basket, promotionId)` in `lib/cart/bonus-product-utils.ts`. Two linear walks (collect the BLIs targeting the promotion, then accumulate `bonusProductsSelected` per BLI in one `productItems` pass) replace the original nested filter+reduce. Consumed via a `useMemo` keyed on `[basket, selectedBonusProduct]` so the walk runs only when the modal is open and is reused across re-renders while it stays open. Drops the unused `maxQuantity` prop from `BonusProductModal` — the modal already derives `remainingCapacity` from the active slot's `maxBonusItems` and `bonusProductsSelected`.

- #1897 `953b850` Thanks @seckardt_sfemu! - fix(cart): deduplicate products sent to CART_MAY_ALSO_LIKE recommender

    The cart loader previously passed `Object.values(productsByItemId)` to the Einstein recommender. Two cart lines mapping to the same parent productId (e.g. two sizes of the same product) would surface that product twice in the input. The loader now dedupes by `product.id` server-side before calling `fetchProductRecommendations`. The dedupe runs in the loader closure and never serializes to the client.

- #1935 `2daacd7` Thanks @seckardt_sfemu! - perf(cart): move rule-based bonus product fetch from on-mount client effect into the cart loader

    Removes the post-hydration client-side `productSearch` waterfall. Rule-based bonus product hits are now fetched server-side by `fetchRuleBasedBonusProductsForBasket` and streamed into `BonusProductSelection` as a deferred `ruleBasedBonusProductsPromise`. SCAPI call count is unchanged (one parallel `productSearch` per rule-based promotion); per-promotion failures still degrade silently to an empty carousel.

    The `<Await>` lives inside `BonusProductSelection` so its outer shell — and the `useFetcher` driving bonus-add — stays mounted across cart-mutating revalidations, and a sized skeleton fallback prevents CLS on small carts. Replaces the client-side `useRuleBasedBonusProducts` hook (deleted) with a `ruleBasedBonusProductsPromise` prop; cart-shell paint timing is unchanged.

- #1982 `e371c42` Thanks @henry-semaganda_sfemu! - fix(bopis): PDP no longer shows "available for pickup" for stores without an inventory list

- #1993 `def83a1` Thanks @seckardt_sfemu! - perf(revalidation): stop re-running the root loader after root-irrelevant mutations

    Add a `shouldRevalidate` to the root route that skips its post-action revalidation for mutations proven to touch no root-loader field — cart, wishlist, promo-code, payment-method, marketing-consent, and pre-auth OTP/password-reset. These previously re-serialized the entire root payload (`appConfig`, `seoMeta`, `clientAuth`, …) for no change.

    The denylist is conservative: navigations, `useRevalidator()` calls, and every unlisted or auth-/currency-/site-context mutation still revalidate, so `clientAuth` and the site context never go stale.

- #1998 `982d795` Thanks @seckardt_sfemu! - perf(revalidation): scope home page loader revalidation

    Add a `shouldRevalidate` to the home route that skips post-action revalidation for non-navigating mutations the home loader does not reflect (add-to-cart, wishlist, promo-code, …), which previously re-issued its fetches for nothing.

    Allowed through, because each changes the loader's output: `set-site-context` (per-currency prices), `update-shopper-context` (feeds every SCAPI response), and `logout` on `/` (re-seeds the auth-dependent wishlist; matched on the `/logout` segment since its action path is site/locale-prefixed).

- #2082 `6d16859` Thanks @seckardt_sfemu! - Share the `shouldRevalidate` ambient-mutation policy across the home and category routes so a suppress-by-default page loader re-runs after a currency/shopper-context change or an auth identity transition (login/signup/logout), re-seeding its auth-dependent wishlist when an identity redirect keeps the route matched. Extract the per-route logic into `src/lib/routes/revalidation/shared.ts`, and have the resource-route fetcher policy (`api-client.ts`) consume the shared context predicate; identity routes stay excluded there because the persistent basket provider re-seeds from the post-merge root-loader snapshot on the redirect, so a fetcher reload would be redundant.

- #2083 `2e4ded5` Thanks @seckardt_sfemu! - Share one `shouldRevalidate` policy between the category and search routes (both loaders are URL- plus site-context-driven), so search now skips post-action revalidation for mutations its loader does not reflect. Extract form-action path normalization into a shared `getActionPath` helper.

- #2084 `7941147` Thanks @seckardt_sfemu! - perf(revalidation): invert the PDP shouldRevalidate to a suppress-by-default allowlist

    The product detail route revalidated after every action and listed the mutations to skip, the inverse of the home and product-listing routes that skip by default and list only the mutations their loader reflects. This aligns the PDP with that model: a non-`GET` submission now skips the expensive loader re-run unless it is proven to change data the loader reads — a context switch (currency / shopper context), an auth identity transition, a store/pickup change, or a review. Every other mutation is skipped; navigation (product, variant, swatch params, explicit `revalidate()`) is unchanged.

- #1997 `bab663a` Thanks @arayanavarro_sfemu! - perf(revalidation): stop re-running the PDP loader after product-irrelevant mutations

    The product detail route had a `shouldRevalidate` that only gated the navigation axis (product/variant changes), so every action submission still re-ran the expensive PDP loader — `fetchProductById` (availability, prices, promotions, variations) plus reviews, category, Page Designer, BNPL, and delivery fetches — even for cart, wishlist, promo-code, payment-method, marketing-consent, and pre-auth OTP/password-reset mutations that touch none of that data.

    Add an action-axis denylist (`PRODUCT_IRRELEVANT_MUTATIONS`) alongside the existing navigation gate. The denylist is conservative: mutations that DO feed the loader — `addReview`, store/pickup changes (availability), the currency switch, and shopper-context updates (pricing/promotions) — stay off the list and still revalidate, and any unlisted mutation revalidates by default.

- #2100 `c980711` Thanks @seckardt_sfemu! - perf(revalidation): scope cart revalidation across the cart route

    The cart route had no `shouldRevalidate`, so its loader re-ran after every action on the page — including a wishlist toggle from a cart line, which re-suspended the basket `<Await>` and orphaned the in-flight wishlist `useFetcher`. The cart loader's content is the basket itself (`getBasket`, in-basket products, promotions, BOPIS pickup stores), so it adopts a revalidate-by-default denylist (like checkout) that skips only the wishlist toggle — tracked client-side and confirmed by the SCAPI audit to touch only `getCustomerProductList(s)`. Cart, promo, bonus, and pickup mutations still revalidate.

- #1990 `c71b6df` Thanks @seckardt_sfemu! - Add `docs/README-REVALIDATION.md` documenting React Router's loader revalidation: when it fires after actions and navigations, why the "revalidate every active loader" default over-fetches at scale (request volume grows with route-tree depth, not change surface), the two ways a re-run is wasteful (no overlap; overlap but value already in the subtree), and how to gate it with `shouldRevalidate`. Move the existing query-param example out of `README-DATA.md` into the new canonical doc and replace it with a pointer; register the doc in the performance topic table and key-documentation indexes.

- #1995 `d4e75e9` Thanks @ssojka_sfemu! - Restore the Page Designer category fallback on variant PDP loads. Variants carry `primaryCategoryId: null`, so the experience lookup (and breadcrumbs) previously dropped the category on a variant load and could no longer fall back to a category-level PDP assignment. The PDP loader now resolves the master product once via a shared promise and feeds the master's `primaryCategoryId` to both the Page Designer experience call and the breadcrumb category fetch — avoiding a redundant master `getProduct` per consumer. Tracked under.

- #2036 `f87e68e` Thanks @jbisa_sfemu! - Fix `my-cart.spec.ts` Before-hook checkout flake by raising `waitForCheckoutReady` default from 30s to 45s and removing the spec-level skip toggle.

- #2015 `f79538c` Thanks @seckardt_sfemu! - Add a `shouldRevalidate` gate to the `resource.reviews-summary` route so the rating-summary fetcher no longer re-runs on unrelated action submissions. The summary is product-global (a pure function of `productId`, independent of auth, currency, shopper-context, selected store, locale, and consent), so while a cart-item or order-line modal is open the fetcher only needs to re-run after an add-review submission. It opts in only when the submission targets `resourceRoutes.addReview` and its result reports success with a new review — every other mutation (add-to-cart, quantity change, set-site-context, update-shopper-context, etc.) now skips the round-trip.

- #2076 `3b07fa3` Thanks @vcua_sfemu! - Add a Cookie Domain Configuration doc

    New `docs/README-COOKIE-DOMAIN.md` covering configurable cookie domains end to end: the storefront config (`app.cookies.domain` + per-site `commerce.sites[].cookies.domain`, precedence, `PUBLIC__app__cookies__domain`), the matching Business Manager setting (Merchant Tools → Site Preferences → Hybrid Auth Settings cookie-domain level), a verification checklist, and rollout guidance. README-AUTH and README-CONFIG now point to it as the canonical home. Adds a `config.server.ts` comment clarifying that the cookie domain is set only via `app.cookies.domain` / per-site overrides (a domain on `app.siteContext.cookieOptions` is ignored).

- #2095 `9438b9d` Thanks @jie-dai_sfemu! - Fix promo codes showing a false "applied" state when valid but ineligible for the cart. Ineligible/rejected codes now surface a status-specific error with the correct 4xx status instead of a phantom discount or a misreported 500.

- #2101 `9cb7384` Thanks @seckardt_sfemu! - perf(revalidation): scope cart revalidation across the basket-products resource

    The always-mounted cart badge kept the `/resource/basket-products` fetcher registered, so React Router revalidated it — a SCAPI round-trip — after every basket mutation, even with the panel closed and nothing rendering its products. Its `shouldRevalidate` now gates the post-action reload on the panel being mounted, and otherwise catches up on the next panel-open or hover/focus prefetch via a staleness check. When a full basket has been loaded into `BasketProvider` (the add/edit/remove handlers and the mini-cart's own publish-back effect write the action-response basket, which carries the SCAPI `lastModified`), the check compares `lastModified` — so a count-neutral change such as a variant swap is detected on reopen. When only the cookie snapshot is available (returning visitor, nothing mutated this session, panel never opened), it falls back to a count-identity diff (`basketId` + total quantity + unique line count), reusing the warm cache when the counts match and reloading when they diverge; a count-neutral cross-tab change is the one narrow gap, and the next route loader reconciles it via the basket middleware.

    The open-panel staleness check is now directional. When the panel is open, `resource.basket-products`' `shouldRevalidate` already revalidates the fetcher after a basket mutation; the `BasketProvider` freshness reference (`useBasket`) trails that fetcher by one render, because the publish-back is a parent `setState` that cannot converge within the same commit. The check compared `lastModified` with a symmetric `!==`, so it read that benign render-lag (fetcher newer than reference) as staleness and fired a redundant second `/resource/basket-products` request. It now reloads only when the reference is strictly newer than the cached data — a revision the cache hasn't pulled yet — and treats an older reference as render-lag, not staleness. `lastModified` is SCAPI's ISO-8601 UTC timestamp, so the lexicographic comparison is chronological.

    The mini-cart's UI state (`open` plus `panelMounted`) moves into one external store (`src/hooks/mini-cart-store.ts`, `useSyncExternalStore` with slice selectors), replacing the former `MiniCartContext` and the standalone visibility flag. It serves both a reactive in-React read and the synchronous read `shouldRevalidate` needs from outside the React tree, and imports only `react` so the route module stays free of client-only dependencies and import cycles.

- #2059 `2e7ec0f` Thanks @clavery_sfemu! - Upgrade `@salesforce/b2c-tooling-sdk` (1.11.0 → 1.15.0) in `storefront-next-dev` and `@salesforce/b2c-cli` (1.12.0 → 1.17.0) in the template. b2c-cli 1.17.0 depends on b2c-tooling-sdk 1.15.0, so both resolve to a single tooling-sdk version. The 1.15.0 release drops the `applicationinsights` dependency, substantially shrinking the transitive tree (removes the Azure Monitor / OpenTelemetry / gRPC packages pulled in transitively). No source changes — the sfnext CLI commands that extend the SDK's base commands (`push`, `config inspect`, `deploy-cartridge`) inherit the updated base behavior automatically.

- #1999 `5a0ef18` Thanks @kumaravinash_sfemu! - perf(revalidation): skip checkout-loader revalidation on step submissions

    Add an intent-based denylist to the checkout route's `shouldRevalidate` policy so the loader does not re-run after step submissions (`contactInfo`, `shippingAddress`, `shippingOptions`, `payment`). Each step action already returns the updated basket in its JSON response and the form page applies it directly via `updateBasket(fetcher.data.basket)`; the loader re-run was redundant SCAPI traffic that roughly doubled the cost of every step submission.

    The pre-existing 3xx-redirect skip and the `framework_skipRevalidation` extension opt-in are preserved unchanged. The policy is moved from inline in `_checkout.checkout.tsx` into `src/lib/routes/revalidation/checkout.ts` for testability and to cluster per-route revalidation policies in one place, matching the pattern established by the root-route policy.

- #1884 `b6c83fb` Thanks @jcesarpierre_sfemu! - Restore Header snapshot coverage by stubbing <Await> in the snapshot harness

- #2033 `2d8baef` Thanks @daniel-diaz_sfemu! - Align the cosmetic vertical's visual design with the Dazzle reference. Replaces the dropped Glow & Co direction (pink/mauve) with Dazzle's sage + cream + warm-beige palette across every token file, and brings borders, shadows, and corner radii to pixel parity. Highlights: byte-aligned color tokens (oklch), `--ui-radius`/`--ui-shadow` shape tokens written to the source vars (not the inlined `@theme` bridge), a `tokens/sidebar.css` for account-nav hover, wider `max-w-[1680px]` editorial container, borderless cards on home/PLP/PDP with delineation borders restored on account/auth/checkout/order-confirmation, a smooth scroll-to-solid header transition with no layout shift, a seamless mega-menu dropdown (squared top corners, single hairline), unified nav-item hover, and a single mini-cart close button. Also adds a `storybook:test:mirror` script that validates a vertical's Storybook snapshots against the flattened mirror output the way CI does (avoiding false failures from running snapshots against the unflattened source tree).

- #2072 `e71ac91` Thanks @daniel-diaz_sfemu! - Fix cosmetic/canonical follow-up bugs surfaced during option-a QA :
    - **Home route 500**: wrap the cosmetic home route in `WishlistProvider` so product-tile `useIsInWishlist()` no longer throws during SSR.
    - **Wishlist hard-throw**: add `errorElement={null}` to the wishlist routes' deferred `<Await>` so a failed product-details fetch degrades gracefully instead of crashing.
    - **Error-page logo**: extract a canonical `@/components/logo` component and render it on the error page so the vertical resolver swaps in the brand (e.g. cosmetic Dazzle) logo; fashion unchanged.
    - **Quick-order modal centering**: stop the cart-item-modal from top-pinning over the base Dialog centering; keep a header-aware max-height for tall/scrollable content.
    - **Quick Add options message desync**: gate the "select all your options" message on the same provider-derived variant that drives `canAddToCart`, so the message and the Add-to-Cart enabled state can no longer disagree (the message previously showed while the button was enabled).

- #2090 `2c96a51` Thanks @jbisa_sfemu! - fix(checkout): keep shopper on Shipping Address after refresh when no valid shipping methods are available for the entered address

- #1969 `d7848b6` Thanks @clavery_sfemu! - fix(page-designer): stop sending both `productId` and `categoryId` to `getPages`

    The PDP route passes a `categoryId` fallback alongside `productId` so the MRT page-resolution middleware can fall back to a category-level PDP assignment. Both values were being forwarded to SCAPI's `/pages` endpoint, which rejects calls with multiple business object IDs (`Business Object ID Invalid`). When the manifest middleware misses (e.g. dev environments without a populated Data Store), the SCAPI fallback produced a 400 instead of resolving the page.

    `fetchPage` now sends `productId` if present, otherwise `categoryId`, otherwise neither. The category fallback is still available to the manifest middleware via the `aspectAttributes` query param.

- #1941 `4987fd3` Thanks @vcua_sfemu! - Fix products with no price for the active currency showing as free and remaining purchasable. When a currency has no price-book entry, SCAPI omits the price field; the storefront previously treated that as `0`, so such products displayed as "$0.00" and could still be added to cart and checked out. Purchasability now requires a price for the active currency (an explicit price of `0` is still purchasable), and the price area shows "Price unavailable" instead of a misleading 0 on catalog surfaces (PLP, PDP, tiles). Promotional bonus products, which are intentionally free, are unaffected.

- #2023 `37d9aee` Thanks @kevin-he_sfemu! - Stop marking cookies `Secure` in local development so Safari can persist them. The template's `getCookieConfig` (auth, basket, shopper-context, turnstile cookies) hardcoded `secure: true`, and the SDK's site-context cookies (`site_id`, `lng`, `currency`) gated `secure` on `NODE_ENV`. Both now gate on a new shared `isRemote()` helper (`@salesforce/storefront-next-runtime/env`, the `BUNDLE_ID` signal) — `true` on deployed Managed Runtime (HTTPS), `false` on local `pnpm dev` and `pnpm preview`.

    Previously, on `http://localhost` Safari/WebKit silently refused to persist `Secure` cookies (Chrome and Firefox mask this with a localhost exception), so no auth cookie ever stuck — sign-in and registration redirected straight back to a guest session. `NODE_ENV` was the wrong signal because `pnpm preview` runs a production build over plain HTTP; `BUNDLE_ID` is the same signal that already gates HSTS and `upgrade-insecure-requests`, so cookies, HSTS, and CSP now agree on "is this real HTTPS?". `SameSite=None` cookies (Page Designer design mode) stay `Secure` regardless, as the spec requires. `isRemote()` is also now exported from the SDK and the security-headers middleware consumes it instead of its own private copy.

- #2000 `d8f9066` Thanks @joel-uong_sfemu! - Type-narrow `getConfig()`'s client-shaped overloads so client callers see a TypeScript error on `.serverExtension`. Mirrors the existing `useConfig()` narrow — the server overload `getConfig(context)` is unchanged. Reuses the SDK's `ClientFacingAppConfigShape` augmentation slot; un-augmented customers see no behavior change on upgrade.

    Wrappers that take `context?` and forward it continue to compile via the maybe-context overload (also narrowed). Server helpers that need the full shape from a maybe-context call should narrow first (`if (ctx) getConfig(ctx)`) or pass `getConfig<AppConfig>(ctx)`.

- #1953 `5bf1ce7` Thanks @jcesarpierre_sfemu! - Fix redundant <MemoryRouter> nested inside global Storybook router decorator

- #2003 `26bacc1` Thanks @j-sheth_sfemu! - Fix hybrid (SFRA/SiteGenesis) handoff to the legacy backend.

    `hybrid.legacyRoutes` entries now accept an optional per-route `suffix` (e.g. `{ pattern: '/product/:id', suffix: '.html' }`) so legacy SEO URLs resolve. Bare-string entries are unchanged and never get a suffix, so clean-path routes like `/cart` stay clean.

    The site-aware `<Link>` now detects legacy routes and performs a full-document navigation (`reloadDocument`) — with the configured suffix applied — instead of relying on the client-side redirect middleware. The middleware-only approach raced React Router's own failed-chunk reload and silently stranded Safari on the current page (e.g. clicking the logo on the cart page did nothing). Handling the handoff at the link level means React Router never starts a client navigation to a legacy route, fixing the cross-browser inconsistency. The middleware remains as a backstop for programmatic `navigate()` calls.

    Removed the `redirected=1` loop-guard query param the middleware previously appended to the redirect URL. It was a PWA Kit holdover (that app's client router re-ran on every load); in this SSR app React Router does not run client middleware on the initial document load, so the loop it guarded against cannot occur. The param leaked to the legacy backend, whose SEO URL rules 404'd on it (e.g. `/category/kitchen?redirected=1`). Redirects now hand the legacy backend a clean URL.

    The legacy-routes middleware now skips Storefront Next infrastructure paths (`/resource/*`, `/action/*`, `/mobify/*`, `/assets/*`, `*.data`, and static assets) before matching. React Router runs client middleware for data/fetcher loads, not just navigations, so a path like `/resource/basket-products` was reaching the matcher; with a `'/:siteId/:localeId'` URL prefix it stripped down to `/`, and when `/` is a configured legacy route the fetcher load triggered a full-page navigation to the legacy backend — bouncing the shopper off Storefront Next with no click. The skip-list mirrors the dev proxy's exclusions and the infrastructure patterns in the eCDN routing rules.

- #1850 `12dbe17` Thanks @joel-uong_sfemu! - Cut `pnpm lint` runtime by disabling `@typescript-eslint/no-misused-promises` `checksVoidReturn.attributes`. The sub-check scans every JSX event-handler attribute (`onClick`, `onChange`, …) against the value's return type, which fans out across thousands of TSX files and pushed `pnpm lint` past 30 minutes on customer 1x CI runners. The rule still fires on argument, property, return, and variable positions, so it continues to catch real misuse — only the JSX-attribute traversal is dropped.

- #1944 `ec62734` Thanks @daniel-diaz_sfemu! - Move einstein-recommenders constants from adapters/engagement to lib/product where the recommendations domain logic lives

- #1913 `8c9ff2f` Thanks @jcesarpierre_sfemu! - Move `.storybook/README-STORYBOOK.md` to `docs/README-STORYBOOK.md` for consistency with other Storybook docs (`README-STORY-COVERAGE.md`, `storybook-review-plan.md`) and update all references

- #2026 `d731250` Thanks @nayana-vishwa_sfemu! - Hide the order confirmation payment card when card details are unavailable. Salesforce Payments orders return no `paymentCard` at page load, which previously rendered a "Salesforce Payments ending in" label with no digits; the section is now hidden unless `paymentCard.cardType` is present.

- #1962 `ad013de` Thanks @joel-uong_sfemu! - fix(login): auto-submit the OTP code on fill and stop the input boxes overflowing the modal

    Two corrections to the OTP verification modal, both surfaced from the checkout flow:
    - **Auto-submit restored.** The modal again submits as soon as the visible slots are completely filled — typed or pasted — instead of requiring a separate **Verify** button click. This matches the checkout UX the modal was originally specified to (one fewer step to log in). Submission fires when the entered code exactly fills the visible slot count, so it never sends a short prefix of a still-being-typed longer code; a code pasted longer than the configured length still expands the boxes and submits whole. The Verify button is removed.
    - **Box overflow fixed.** Each slot was a fixed 48px wide, so an 8-slot row (an 8-digit OTP length) ran past the dialog's right edge — the boxes were clipped on the checkout modal. The slots are now capped at the same design width but shrink to fit, so a full 8-box row stays inside the modal at every viewport. (The overflow predated this change; it was only visible once an OTP length produced 8 boxes.)

    The OTP length itself is delivered by SLAS, not the storefront — `config.auth.otpLength` is only the storefront's guess and can drift from what SLAS sends. Aligning the configured length with the SLAS-issued length is tracked separately with the SLAS team.

- #1903 `f50c8d6` Thanks @joel-uong_sfemu! - fix(login): tolerate an OTP length mismatch at the verification modal

    `config.auth.otpLength` is the storefront's configured guess at the OTP length, but SLAS owns the length it actually delivers (it emails the code straight to the shopper — the request contract carries no length field), so the two can drift. Previously the modal rendered exactly `otpLength` single-digit slots and only submitted once all of them filled, so a client whose SLAS OTP is longer than the configured length stranded the shopper: they physically could not enter the extra digits.

    The modal now degrades gracefully across the 6–8 digit range SLAS issues:
    - It renders `otpLength` slots by default but **expands to fit a longer pasted code**, up to the 8-digit SLAS maximum — so the box count matches the code the shopper actually received (6 boxes for a 6-digit code, 8 for an 8-digit one).
    - A typed code submits via an explicit **Verify** button (enabled at 6–8 gapless digits); a **pasted** code auto-submits, since a paste arrives whole and can't be a prefix of a longer code.

    The default `otpLength` stays **6** (unchanged from `main`).

    **Why it's built this way:**
    - _Default stays 6._ Six digits is the industry norm and product's intended default; new SLAS clients move to 6-digit during the Fast Storefront setup work, so the storefront default and SLAS converge on 6. This change does not touch the default — it makes the modal resilient to the transitional period (and any future drift) where a client's SLAS OTP length doesn't match the configured value.
    - _Expand on paste, not "always render 8."_ The storefront cannot know the delivered length ahead of time, so it cannot pre-render the correct number of boxes. Always rendering 8 would leave two permanently-empty boxes for every 6-digit shopper and contradict the "we sent a 6-digit code" copy. Growing to fit the pasted code keeps the box count honest and the common case clean.
    - _Auto-submit on paste only, not on type._ Auto-submitting the moment a **typed** code hits the configured length would fire a 6-digit prefix of a longer code before the shopper finished — re-creating the block as a rejection loop, because a typed code's final length is unknowable mid-entry. A **paste** has no such ambiguity (it arrives whole), so a pasted 6-to-8-digit code auto-submits; typed codes confirm with Verify.
    - _Known limitation (by design):_ a code longer than `otpLength` that is **typed by hand** rather than pasted won't expand the inputs — auto-growing on each keystroke would show a spurious empty box to every correctly-configured shopper, a worse regression than the gap. OTPs are nearly always pasted/autofilled; the shopper can also paste or correct the config.

    Also removes the unused `react-hook-form`/`zod` scaffolding from the modal (it was never wired to submission) and the orphaned `login.otpRequired` / `login.otpInvalidFormat` strings.

- #1869 `592b2b6` Thanks @joel-uong_sfemu! - Format the generated page-designer static registry with the project's Prettier config so it is no longer reformatted on every commit. Previously the generator emitted trailing whitespace and over-width `registerImporter` lines, so a standalone `prettier --write` rewrote the file on every commit. The generated file is now also exempted in the ESLint config alongside other generated artifacts, and the redundant `/* eslint-disable */` it used to emit is dropped.

- #1984 `6b2c945` Thanks @vmarta_sfemu! - Pass `--coverage` to `vitest run` in the template's `pnpm test` script so the coverage thresholds defined in `vitest.config.ts` are actually enforced when the script runs in CI. Without the flag Vitest skipped the coverage pass entirely, leaving the configured thresholds (`{ lines: 73, statements: 73, functions: 72, branches: 67 }`) unchecked on every PR.

- #1936 `6e98953` Thanks @kumaravinash_sfemu! - Fix two checkout Turnstile optimizations:
    - Stop rendering a second Turnstile widget at the "Save for Future Use" step; rely on the server-side `cc-tv` cookie short-circuit in `enforceTurnstile` so subsequent Turnstile-protected actions in the same checkout flow skip re-verification
    - Gate the Continue button on contact-info on `form.formState.isValid` so an incomplete form cannot be submitted, and remove the misleading "verification refreshed" toast on Turnstile timeout (Cloudflare auto-refreshes silently)

- #2060 `3ed5c33` Thanks @joel-uong_sfemu! - Tighten the `/wishlist` and `/account/wishlist` `shouldRevalidate` gate to the ambient-mutations allowlist shared by the home route — the loader now re-runs only on `setSiteContext` (currency switch refreshes per-currency tile prices), `updateShopperContext` (cross-cutting input to every SCAPI response), and auth identity transitions (login / signup / logout re-seed the auth-dependent wishlist for the new identity). Add-to-cart from a wishlist tile, wishlist remove, payment changes, and account-preference mutations no longer re-fire `getCustomerProductLists` and the per-item product lookups for state the page doesn't render.

- Updated dependencies [`ebae987`, `bc6204f`, `25272e5`, `35d961b`, `ec0a077`, `2e7ec0f`, `8874f57`, `c0b87fb`, `b80d30d`, `6c57057`, `c55c95b`, `f7786e4`, `9d7cf52`, `75f6dc2`, `37d9aee`, `d8f9066`, `e38ea13`, `39b9c15`, `4fe15ff`, `4343407`, `592b2b6`, `ea714d4`, `43ba4ba`]:
